title: Highest temperature in Sao Paulo on June 15?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders have converged on 19°C as the highest temperature in São Paulo on June 15, 2026, reflecting real-time meteorological data and forecast consensus from agencies such as INMET and international models. June marks the Southern Hemisphere winter, with typical highs near 21–23°C moderated by cooler Atlantic air masses and possible light rain or cloud cover that caps afternoon warming. Recent observations show daytime temperatures climbing steadily into the upper teens Celsius without exceeding this threshold, aligning precisely with the market-implied outcome. This positioning rests on verified station readings rather than projections. Unlikely challenges include localized sensor discrepancies or rapid frontal shifts altering the final maximum, though current model agreement and elapsed daytime hours make such revisions improbable.
